steps.title: Use the zero product property
steps.content: A product equals zero when at least one factor equals zero. Set each factor in $(x+2)(x-5)(x+9)=0$ equal to zero.

steps.title: Find the roots
steps.content: $x+2=0$ gives $x=-2$, $x-5=0$ gives $x=5$, and $x+9=0$ gives $x=-9$. On a graph, these are the x-intercepts.

steps.title: Choose the positive solution
steps.content: The only positive solution is $5$, so choice C is correct. The negative roots are valid solutions to the equation, but they do not answer the question asked.
